metric: Federal public laws enacted per calendar year
provenance: GovInfo PLAW (Public and Private Laws) collection sitemaps, one per year: https://www.govinfo.gov/sitemap/PLAW_{YEAR}_sitemap.xml . Counted unique package IDs matching PLAW-{congress}publ{n}; 'pvtl' (private law) packages were excluded. Keyless/no API key required.
notes: GovInfo buckets each law by its enactment (signing) date, giving a true calendar-year basis that aligns with the Federal Register and CFR series. CROSS-CHECKED against the Senate 'Resume of Congressional Activity' PDFs (https://www.senate.gov/legislative/ResumesofCongressionalActivity1947present.htm, 'Public bills enacted into law' = Senate column + House column), which are SESSION-based rather than calendar-based. The two agree exactly where sessions align (2009: 125 both) and diverge exactly as expected where they do not: the Senate's 2010 session count (258) exceeds the calendar-2010 count (221) by 37, and the calendar-2011 sitemap contains exactly 37 laws from the 111th Congress (signed in early January 2011 after the lame-duck session) — a precise reconciliation confirming the calendar-year semantics. Congress-total sanity checks also pass (106th: 580 expected). Note the Senate resume PDFs are only available from 2009 onward; requests for 1999-2008 return an HTTP 200 HTML soft-404, not a PDF, so that route could not supply the early years and GovInfo was used as the primary source throughout. The Congress.gov API was not used: it requires a key, none was found in the environment or macOS Keychain, and DEMO_KEY returned OVER_RATE_LIMIT. 2026 is a partial year: data retrieved 2026-07-19 and covers only part of the year. Do not compare it to full years.
